player_utterance: Quiet out here. You doing alright?

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the damp bench, paper cup in one hand, phone face-down in the other

player_utterance: Long enough to know that "alright" can mean just about anything on a night like this.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the damp bench, shifting his weight, looking at the candle on the small table

player_utterance: Did you know him well — the man you're all here for tonight?

believer_state_after.posture: standing, one hand resting on the back of the damp bench, looking past you toward the lit windows of the house

player_utterance: That gesture — what were you reaching for up there?

believer_state_after.posture: still standing, but his hand drops to his side; he glances up briefly, then back at you

player_utterance: And with that check, what changed — just the roof, or something else too?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the damp bench, program flat between his palms, looking down at it

voice_offers.read: He glanced up; the gesture meant something he can't name yet.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to answer. I just noticed — that's all.

voice_offers.read: Press the specific motion; vague comfort lets him retreat into liturgy.
voice_offers.line: Your fingers moved like you were starting a cross and stopped. Lutherans don't, usually. So what was that?

player_utterance: The questions stopped — and you were one of the people who had been asking them.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, elbows on knees, hands loose between them, looking at the ground

voice_offers.read: He won't answer aloud; the silence is doing our work.
voice_offers.line: You haven't said one word since I sat down. That's an answer too, isn't it?

voice_offers.read: Three questions, three silences — pick the one he can't dodge.
voice_offers.line: The check. You wrote it before or after he died? That part I can't tell from how you're sitting.

player_utterance: Whatever stopped you from asking — I don't think it was weakness. But I hear you carrying it like it was.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, has picked the program back up, turning it slowly in his hands

voice_offers.read: He's silent because the answer indicts him. Name it.
voice_offers.line: You took their questions to that roof with you, didn't you. And then you stopped asking too.

voice_offers.read: He's looking at the ground, not away. Let him find it.
voice_offers.line: Whatever you were asking up there — I'm not here to grade it.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, the program flat on his knee now, smoothing it with the heel of his hand

voice_offers.read: He's turning the program; the Seeker just named the wound kindly.
He's quoting because speaking costs too much; press the silence itself.
voice_offers.line: You're turning that program like it'll answer for you. It won't. I'm asking you.

player_utterance: What did you ask, up on that roof?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, the program still flat on his knee, looking up past the lit windows into the darker sky above the house

voice_offers.read: He's smoothing the program; the silence is doing work.
Three silences in. He won't break himself. Name it.
voice_offers.line: You climbed up there with their questions in your pocket. What did you ask, and what answered you?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, hands loose between his knees, looking at nothing in particular

voice_offers.read: He's reaching for sky, not hiding; let the question stand.
He's gathering himself to deflect upward; cut it off.
voice_offers.line: Not what a Lutheran is supposed to ask. What did *you* ask, John?

player_utterance: Take your time. I'm not going anywhere.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, head tipped back slightly, looking up at the dark above the courtyard

voice_offers.read: Three silences in. He's drifting, not opening. Press now.
voice_offers.line: John. The roof. What did you ask. Not Luther — you.

voice_offers.read: He's sitting with it; my hand should rest, not hover.
voice_offers.line: Take your time. I'm not going anywhere.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, both hands resting on his thighs, very still

voice_offers.read: He's looking up; the silence is doing its work.
Patience has been given twice; the question still hangs.
voice_offers.line: You're looking up like something's still up there. What did you ask it, John?

player_utterance: Is this the first time you've said any of this out loud?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, elbows on knees, hands clasped, head lowered

voice_offers.read: Three silences offered, three taken; the question still hangs unanswered.
voice_offers.line: John. I've given you all the room there is. What did you ask up there?

voice_offers.read: He quotes Luther to avoid speaking himself; pin him to the gap.
voice_offers.line: You keep giving me Luther's words. I'm asking what *yours* were, on that roof.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, hands clasped tighter now, looking at the candle flame on the table

voice_offers.read: He's already breaking; the question itself is enough.
He's down. One quiet push and he says it.
voice_offers.line: What did you ask, John. Out loud. Now.

player_utterance: And you don't feel you've earned that yet.

believer_state_after.posture: head down, hands pressed to his face, elbows still on his knees

voice_offers.read: He's breaking; the question already landed.
He's almost there; one more nudge finishes it.

believer_state_after.posture: still sitting, but his hands have come down to rest on his thighs; he is looking at the candle on the table

voice_offers.read: He's already there. Pushing now would be cruelty for its own sake.
He's almost spoken it. One quiet nudge finishes the turn.
voice_offers.line: Say it, John. Whatever you asked up there — say it now.
